title: map, unordered_map and set

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for map, unordered_map and set; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Standard C++ Tour; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to map, unordered_map and set
sources.claim: Ordered associative containers use key ordering, while unordered containers use hash and equality; sets store keys and maps associate unique keys with mapped values.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Ordered associative containers use key ordering, while unordered containers use hash and equality; sets store keys and maps associate unique keys with mapped values.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A map counts words but uses find when a lookup must not create a missing entry, reserving operator[] for intentional insertion or update. Your goal is to choose and use an associative container while explaining ordering and expected lookup cost. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Loops and range iteration; Copy and assignment semantics; string, array and vector. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: Ordered associative containers use key ordering, while unordered containers use hash and equality; sets store keys and maps associate unique keys with mapped values. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Custom comparison must be strict weak ordering, hash must agree with equality, insertion can invalidate unordered iterators during rehash, and operator[] can insert. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: map operator[] is a read-only lookup that leaves the container unchanged when a key is absent.

includes: Use ordered and hashed associative containers with suitable keys and operations.
Choose and use an associative container while explaining ordering and expected lookup cost.
excludes: Custom comparison must be strict weak ordering, hash must agree with equality, insertion can invalidate unordered iterators during rehash, and operator[] can insert.

outcomes: Choose and use an associative container while explaining ordering and expected lookup cost.

examples.title: map, unordered_map and set: defined case
examples.situation: A map counts words but uses find when a lookup must not create a missing entry, reserving operator[] for intentional insertion or update.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A map counts words but uses find when a lookup must not create a missing entry, reserving operator[] for intentional insertion or update.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: map operator[] inserts a value-initialized mapped value for a missing key; use find, contains, or at when insertion is not intended.
examples.result: The defined result follows because map operator[] inserts a value-initialized mapped value for a missing key; use find, contains, or at when insertion is not intended.

examples.title: map, unordered_map and set: boundary case
examples.situation: An unordered_set custom key supplies equality and a consistent hash so equal keys always hash equally.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: An unordered_set custom key supplies equality and a consistent hash so equal keys always hash equally.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Custom comparison must be strict weak ordering, hash must agree with equality, insertion can invalidate unordered iterators during rehash, and operator[] can insert.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: map operator[] is a read-only lookup that leaves the container unchanged when a key is absent.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Choose and use an associative container while explaining ordering and expected lookup cost.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model map, unordered_map and set: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
